title: Agent Coordination + brain→memory migration
label: agent-coordination
purpose: Prevent parallel-implementation conflicts across agent surfaces (Claude Code, Cowork, Codex) and consolidate the cowork .brain/ knowledge base into the canonical memory/ pipeline so there is exactly one source of truth.
why_now: On 2026-05-05 the same cross-session memory problem was solved twice in parallel: cowork built `.brain/`, Claude Code built `memory/<type>/<id>.md`. The duplication only became visible after both branches existed. We need a coordination gate up front and the duplicate content reconciled.

origin.summary: Two agent surfaces independently shipped competing memory stores. Decision: keep canonical memory/ pipeline, migrate .brain/ entries in as lifecycle:candidate for human review, install a check-in gate to prevent recurrence.

success_signal: (1) `node scripts/agent-checkin.js --intent "..."` exits 2 when an active branch holds an overlapping scope. (2) AGENT-COORDINATION.md lists every active branch with surface + scope-locks. (3) All .brain entries appear under memory/<type>/ with lifecycle:candidate and source_capture=brain-migration-2026-05-05. (4) Both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md instruct agents to run agent-checkin.js before non-trivial work.
non_goals: Auto-resolving overlap (humans/agents resolve, the script only detects)
Replacing .wolf/ context tooling — coexists with it
Promoting candidate entries — that goes through the existing memory-promote flow

workstreams.title: AGENT-COORDINATION.md — single source of truth
acceptance: Active branches table populated for the three current branches.

workstreams.title: scripts/agent-checkin.js — pre-flight gate
acceptance: Detects overlap by branch name, scope-lock, and changed-file keyword match.

workstreams.title: Wire the check-in rule into CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
acceptance: Both docs reference scripts/agent-checkin.js and AGENT-COORDINATION.md.

workstreams.title: Per-surface branch naming convention
acceptance: Defer until 2+ agent surfaces ship a second collision; current 3-branch sample is too small to lock convention.

mvp: ws_coordination_doc + ws_checkin_script + ws_brain_migration + ws_rule_integration land in this branch.
decisions: Scope-locks are declared *manually* in AGENT-COORDINATION.md; the check-in script reads them, it does not invent them.
agent-checkin.js is the gate, not a pre-commit hook — agents call it explicitly so the act of coordination is intentional.
.brain entries land as lifecycle:candidate so humans review before they become 'active' canonical memory.
